(What is this? Antarctica's essence? Its truth? Its... future?)
Yet again, thoughts formed from the blank space in the back of her mind.
Yes. This is my story. One of the possible futures for this continent.
Phew...
(Was I just hallucinating? Everything is back to normal, looking as it should...)
Hum-de-dum♪
Pamiat', what exactly did you mean by "your story"?
My "story"? What are you talking about?
Me and Ognevoy were just having a chat. You must've misheard something I said.
Misheard? But...
Are you alright, Soyuz? Are you hearing things?
Not just hearing things. I saw something, too.
Audiovisual hallucinations, eh... You did touch that crystal earlier. Maybe that has something to do with it?
Could be! Her very rigging did get restructured. That takes a huge toll on your body and mind.
Yes... You're probably right.
Are you sure you're okay? Should we stop and take a break?
I'm fine, I promise. The base isn't far now, so let's get out of the cold.

The Snowmelt Area.say: Iron Blood's base was on high alert, ready to respond quickly in case of an enemy invasion.
Getting inside would've been difficult for Soyuz's party, but luckily, a message from Tallinn allowed them inside without much fuss.
Greetings. I am Königsberg, the head of operations at this base.
Allow me to sincerely thank you, Sovetsky Soyuz and company, for offering us your assistance.
You don't need to thank us. What's the situation?
You've generously come to our aid, and so the least I can do is tell you the facts as they are.
To be honest with you, the situation is quite bad.
(What now? Soyuz said she wanted to do some reconnaissance...)
(Shh! Don't say everythin' out loud!)
As I wrote in my message to you, we have lost contact with the research station near the snowmelt area.
The last message we got from them was, "Engaging unidentified enemy."
Shortly after, the signal started being jammed, and we haven't heard anything more from them.
To make matters worse, we also lost contact with our nearest supply base. The jamming cut us off from them, too.
Due to all these problems, I'm afraid we're in no position to provide any hospitality.
I wasn't expecting your group to come all the way out here to speak to us, let alone offer us help.
I see you've also gotten a new rigging, Soyuz... Congratulations.
What brought all of you to Antarctica, anyway? The new crystal that was discovered in the snowmelt area?
I see word about it reached you quickly.
(That confirms it. It WAS the crystal that the Tribunal was talking about.)
Indeed, it was. However, I also wanted to investigate the phenomena that arose following its discovery.
As you know, abnormal weather is often an omen of an impending Siren attack.
Even ignoring that, it's extremely likely that the unidentified enemy is part of a plot against you.
Perhaps it's a Reenactment, perhaps it's a conspiracy...
Whatever the case, I'd like to know how Iron Blood plans to respond. What will you do?
It depends. We've dispatched a team to the site. What they report back will decide our next move.
If I may ask, what do YOU plan to do?
We are going to head straight for the snowmelt area and conduct an investigation.
It's clear that the abnormal weather and the unidentified enemy are linked to the crystal.
Therefore, a direct investigation will hopefully bring a swift end to this situation.
That's quite brave of you.
I sadly don't have any intel to share on the mysterious enemy, but I will say this...
Please be careful out there.
If I find out anything new, I'll contact you immediately.
Thank you. Likewise, we will let you know if we find anything of note.
Also, I have a proposition to make.
Given that Antarctica is fairly isolated from any faction's sphere of influence, it's hard to argue humanity has a strong presence here.
In the event that a problem arises, I believe all factions present should unite to deal with it.
To facilitate this, I propose an agreement to share what information we gather with other factions as well.
I'm sure that, if present here, Comrade Commander would've suggested the same thing.
Sure. I'm okay with that.
To think you brought up the Commander... Well, I suppose hearing it does convince people.
I'd also like to propose an evacuation plan, in case of a disaster.
We have a special submarine that has the capacity to evacuate all the personnel stationed here.
If we can control the situation, I would like this place to serve as the stronghold from which we conduct all our plans to neutralize the enemy.
But, in the worst-case scenario, I suggest that everyone stationed here gathers at our research station. Then we may evacuate using our submarine.
That is very generous of you.
To tell you the truth, this fortress relies almost entirely on automated defense systems. I'm the sole shipgirl stationed here.
Defending this place without the prospect of reinforcements is, frankly, a bad idea.
To put it another way, this is not a hill worth dying on.
I agree to your evacuation plan.
Should this situation develop into a crisis, I will head straight to your research station.
Thank you. I'll tell Tallinn so she can make arrangements for your arrival in advance.
That should be everything, I think. We will now make our way to the snowmelt area.
Good luck. Do teach the enemy a thing or two about attacking us.

sequence: En Route to the Snowmelt Area

Under the Surface.say: Pfft...
Ahahah! I can't believe Königsberg believed that!
She really thought Soyuz's rigging just got a little upgrade, rather than being rebuilt from scratch using that crystal!
I bet she'd also believe it if we said this gear is all mining and survey gear!
It didn't seem like she knew the Eagle Union had dispatched a huge fleet either.
Either the signal jamming has left her completely clueless, or she was hiding something from us.
Then again, Iron Blood's base is the closest to the crystals, so maybe she's just been blinded by overconfidence.
...You know, I still can't believe the Eagle Union sent such a large fleet. And not just mass-produced ships either, but shipgirls.
Soyuz, I know there's something you're not telling us.
This isn't just about the crystals. You're looking for something else near the snowmelt area.
...So you realized.
I knew something was up. You wouldn't travel across the world with a nonfunctional rigging just to see a quarry.
Tell us already. What ARE you here for?
I'm sorry. I should have told you sooner. Königsberg, too.
In fact, I was supposed to explain this shortly after we arrived on the continent.
This led to that, and, well... But you've brought it up now, and we have some time before we reach our destination.
Let me start by saying that the Eagle Union almost certainly doesn't know what I'm about to tell you.
There is a shelter beneath the snowmelt area. Its construction was a joint effort between us and Iron Blood.
It is a doomsday bunker a thousand meters below the surface. It was built to protect all the people inside no matter what calamity was unfolding on the surface.
Its name is, "The Ring of the Wise."
Naturally, I have no reason to believe it had anything to do with the discovery of the rigging-enhancing crystal...
However, it might just be the reason why the snow on the surface has started melting.
I'll be damned.
A bunker of ours was responsible for the snowmelt.
Königsberg seemed completely unaware of it. I take it this is highly classified intel, then?
It is. I suspect that even you had never heard a word about it until now.
As it should be. Owing to its secret nature, only a very small handful of shipgirls know about its existence.
A bunker's the cause of it, eh... I thought we had another unsolvable case on our hands.
Happens all too often that a cool mystery has a boring explanation.
Let's not jump to conclusions. We don't yet KNOW if the Ring created the snowmelt area.
It shouldn't be able to. It's in a dormant state right now.
The only way it could cause snowmelt on this scale is if someone deliberately activated and modified certain facilities.
Then there is the unidentified enemy and the crystal. Those obviously weren't there during the construction or planning stage.
(If other factions are allowed to investigate freely, it's only a matter of time before they discover the bunker.)
(Even if the Commander covers for us, Naval HQ will nail us for it.)
We have to get to the bottom of all this. This is one mission I cannot sit out on.

sequence: Site of Unusual Weather - Snowmelt Area

Scale the Mountains.say: Hey... Does anyone else hear that? There's a river nearby!
That ain't no river, that's a waterfall! Multiple of 'em, even!
How the hell is there running water this near the South Pole?
It's the snowmelt, no? It's revealing the mountains underneath.
Tallinn wasn't kidding. I know she wasn't, but now that I'm seeing it with my own eyes...
It's not just melting the snow on the surface. Underground rivers are forming inside the mountain... The bunker has to be doing this.
Please be careful. The ice has become unstable.
Watch your footing as we ascend this mountain.
Ugh! Why does our adventure in the snow have to include climbing a mountain?! I'm so tireeed!
*pant*... *pant*...
This sucks ass... Shipgirls were made to travel seas, not mountainsides!
Oh! Svirepy, look at that glowy stuff over there! I think that's the mineral we came here for!
A crystal jutted out from the mountain. Surrounded by black rock, its blue glow was unmistakable.
That blue light... Interesting. It's small, but this shows that there are deposits of it even on the surface.
Huh... It's surrounded by ice. Ya think it's got some kinda ambient coolin' effect?
Wow! It's like a fridge, but prettier!
You could put it in a cooler box with some ice cream and take it anywhere without it melting!
We should bring a bunch home. So long, refrigerators – your services are no longer needed!
Come on, people. Refrigeration is not what we want them for. These are rigging superchargers.
Just look at what one did to Soyuz's rigging. If we study them, we can give all of you fancy new riggings.
New riggings? Oh, hell yeah, baby.
So if I touch it, will it restructure mine, too?
Wait! Don't–
Soyuz's words weren't quick enough. Svirepy picked up the fist-sized crystal and held it with both hands.
W-well? How do you feel, Svirepy?!
Oooowch! My frickin' hands! S-so cold!
Ow... Besides giving me frostbite, I don't think it did anything. I still feel the same.
No surprise there. Tallinn already said touching it doesn't really do anything.
Maybe it needs to be bigger. Or some condition have to be met? Who knows what you need to do!
We don't have time for this right now. Let's leave that crystal behind.
The snowmelt has reached much further than I initially expected.
We need to get inside the Ring ASAP and investigate what's wrong with it.
You said it's, like, a thousand meters underground, right? Shouldn't we be goin' DOWN, not up?
It is deep down, yes, but the entrance is up there. The rock provides a stable foundation and a mountain is a good landmark for finding it amid all this snow and ice.
Hasn't the snowmelt, y'know, changed the terrain? Like, a lot? You sure you remember where the entrance is supposed to be?
...No, I'm afraid I don't.
You DON'T?! So what are we gonna do? Search the whole mountain from top to bottom?!
That won't be necessary. There's an identification device near the entrance. If it's active, it should respond to this transmitter I have.
Remember, be on your guard for the as-of-yet unidentified enemy.
The search for the entrance commences now.

The Ring of the Wise.say: After some searching, Soyuz successfully located the entrance to the bunker.
The party caught their breaths after all that mountain-climbing before proceeding inside.

sequence: The Ring of the Wise - Bunker Entrance

The Ring of the Wise.say: The sound of gears grinding together. Slowly, the steel blast doors opened like the jaws of some giant.
Inside, they didn't find what they were expecting. Not a corridor large enough to drive a tank through, no winding paths reminiscent of a maze, not even cutting-edge machinery.
All there was was an office. In addition to all the usual furnishings, it was covered in a layer of dust, having not been used for so long.
This... is lame as hell.
I imagined somethin' way cooler when you called this a "doomsday bunker."
I mean, come on! This looks like any old naval facility I've been to.
Aha, I've got it! It was designed to look boring!
Because it looks so boring, people who don't know any better will go, "Meh, just another Northern Parliament base," and write it off!
But that's exactly what we want them to think. There's gotta be another entrance to the REAL bunker, right?
...Yes, you are correct. This office is only a facade.
The real entrance is this way. Come with me.
To the surprise of nobody, there was a hidden space behind the bookcase. It moved aside, revealing the true entrance.
After traversing a long corridor and passing through many blast-resistant roller shutters, the girls finally arrived at an elevator that would take them down to the bunker.
All we need to do is get off at the right floor and we'll gain access to the Ring.
Wait, the "right" floor? What happens if we get off on the wrong one?
You don't want to know.
C'mon, you HAVE to admit THIS is overkill. This bunker is hidden in the middle of nowhere on a barren continent, behind layers of security and facades...
Do you NEED a trapped elevator, too? Nobody's gonna breach this.
Wrong. Not only has someone already breached it, they have also already activated the bunker's facilities.
Have you noticed how the temperature has been slowly rising?
I have! It's warm and cozy in here! Just like back at the research station!
Oh, yeah, it's not cold anymore. Happened so gradually I didn't even notice...
Yes, the heating has been running since long before we arrived here. The temperature wouldn't be so stable if it hadn't.
However, the snowmelt on the scale we saw outside couldn't possibly have happened simply by someone starting up the bunker's systems.
Someone, for some reason, activated some part of this facility. This has been all but confirmed at this point.
Please stick together once we're inside. We're heading to the security room first of all.
Roger!
The elevator looked quite old, and yet to everyone's surprise, it brought them to the bunker's interior both faster and quieter than they thought.
Sheesh! Did you see how fast that was?!
Huh. Guess this place isn't just like ANY old bunker.
The architect may have had fun with its design, but it was built with utility first in mind.
This was all cutting-edge tech at the time. The only quaint thing about it is its looks.
Uh-huh... So we're a thousand meters deep now, yeah?
Doubt it. That was only about twenty storeys. I think we'll have to change to another mode of transport.
Correct. We will need to pass through a few more in-between areas before we finally enter the main part of the bunker.
The path will take us through many layers of security and multiple elevators.
One of which is the area up ahead.
I will go into the security room and gather the info we need. The rest of you, please wait here.
A while later, Soyuz returned to her waiting comrades.
Took you a while. Did you have trouble finding the stuff?
No, it actually went extremely well. There were some interesting things on the security logs.
Unfortunately... they were also quite confusing.
According to the logs, the bunker has been active since long before the snowmelt area appeared.
Beyond the dwelling area, a few manufacturing areas were activated as well. However, there were no logs of what was manufactured.
Additionally, the shelter's control system has been scanning all this time and still is. It's generated an enormous amount of reports.
I tried to see who ordered the scan, but this was also not in the data logs.
All security systems – cameras included – haven't detected anything, much less sounded an alarm.
Assuming an intruder is behind this, they know this place inside out.
They have to, since they had the high clearance needed to activate the facilities AND erased all trace of themselves having been here.
So someone who has high clearance and knows about this shelter... Could it be someone from the Intelligence Bureau?
That's absolutely impossible. I am the only person who knows how to delete records from the database.
Then maybe someone hacked their way in?
Melting the ice, running the scans... What if the intruder was using the bunker to look for veins of that crystal?
That's... plausible. Let us look for evidence.
There are two areas to prioritize.
First, the manufacturing area – the intruder made something there. Second, the data center – where you can view the results of all those scans.
Of course, we have areas further down to explore, but let's take things one at a time.
Proceed with caution and keep your eyes peeled.

Ice, Rock, and Sand.say: The shipgirls proceeded deeper into the shelter.
As Soyuz said, they had to traverse through many security areas and ride several elevators.
Then, they followed a long corridor and boarded a shuttle system.
Now, at long last, they had reached their destination – the manufacturing area.
Urgh, this place is drivin' me nuts...
It's one thing to have security checkpoints between areas and multiple elevators, but a frickin' SHUTTLE?! Come on, you must be joking!
Listen, this isn't some small missile silo. It's an enormous complex.
If you could just ride an elevator all the way down to the bottom, you might as well get rid of all the security 'cause it could be so easily bypassed.
Getting confused by the bunker's layout? Guess what, that's on purpose. If you feel lost, imagine how lost an intruder would feel.
Good point. Breaking in would be one thing, but breaking out would be much harder.
I'm... I'm running out of breath here...
I've never walked underground this long before... I miss seeing the sky...
Hang in there. We're almost at the underground cave system.
That is where the heart of the bunker is located. We can take a break there.
Wait... underground cave system?
Yup! People have been saying for a long time that there's a sea underneath Antarctica's ice sheets.
Come again? An UNDERGROUND sea?!
Yes, it's true.
From a geographical viewpoint, the possibility of a subglacial sea in Antarctica has long been theorized.
Then, when we constructed this bunker, we found actual evidence for the theory.
You'll see it for yourself once we arrive at the Ring.
First, though, we're going to find out what the intruder made in the manufacturing area.
The production line is just up ahead. Let's hurry.
The girls arrived in front of a roller shutter in the hallway connecting to the manufacturing area. As soon as it opened, a cold wind rushed out.
Achoo! Sh-shit, that's cold!
We're still underground, ain't we? Why isn't the AC workin'?
This place is being cooled, not heated. Strange. It shouldn't be too cold at this depth, but it's as chilly as it was on the surface.
It's a production feature. The environmental temperature is optimized over in manufacturing, so it's not surprising that the temperature may not be very comfortable.
Anyway, let's turn off the cooling and switch on the lights.
The lamps came on in sequence, casting a warming light over the dark area, slowly revealing more and more of it.
Before them was Production Line HL-17-36.
This was but one of hundreds of manufacturing lines inside the bunker, and its scale was on par with that of a standard factory. A Zemlya Design Bureau factory, to be precise.
Originally, this line was designed to produce mainly land vehicles for traversing ice fields and machine parts.
That was not what was being produced now, however. The production line was creating something else and transporting it up into the bunker's superstructure.
What on Earth is going on here?
The machines are taking ice, rock, and sand... and using them to create those crystals!
The crystals are manmade?! How is that possible?!
This doesn't make any sense...
Those crystals discovered in the snowmelt area... they came from here? They were made in a bunker? A bunker that WE built?
And outta ice and rock, too!
How the hell do you create crystals out of rock and ice?!
Soyuz, are these crystals the real deal? They can't be, right?
This has to be alchemy... Or black magic, or something!
No... These crystals are genuine. The production line has been repurposed to produce them through some mysterious means.
It doesn't seem like we'll learn how, either. The manufacturing machines have all been destroyed from the inside, and there are no logs left...
Destroyed from the inside? When?
The moment we entered this area, I believe. Most likely, the change in temperature served as a trigger for the logs and machines to self-destruct.
I'm afraid that we won't find anything useful here beyond more crystal samples.
With a sudden loud bang, the production line came to a complete stop.
My hopes were low, but I didn't expect we'd have THIS poor luck...
Ognevoy might be right. What happened here falls in the domain of magic.
Yes, I can't begin to grasp it either. All we know is that it DID happen.
Through some means, the ice and rock were combined to form the crystals, which were then carried to the surface.
We will need to conduct research into how this was done, and how the mechanism behind the rigging resonance works.
We didn't find much here, so it's time to try the data center. I hope we'll find what we're looking for there.

The Wise Man's Masterworks.say: The elevator's descent slowed, gently stopping at the correct floor.
That isn't to say they had reached the data center, only that they had reached a short hallway connecting to the next elevator.
The only thing setting this cold, boring corridor apart from all others they had seen was the numbers painted on the wall.
They expected more of the same beyond the next door. They were wrong.
This time, something was different.
Huh? This is new. This corridor is furnished with pretty wallpaper.
And there's a nice carpet, a chandelier, even expensive furniture...
Interesting. This must be where they keep their best stuff.
Soyuz, does this bunker have an art storage area? For preserving important works of art, I mean.
It's... not a stretch to say it has something similar to that.
But it isn't this place, and the art certainly wouldn't be out on display.
The art could have been relocated, but if it was, this isn't where it would all go.
The shipgirls looked down the long corridor and saw a few paintings adorning the walls. It was almost like an art gallery.
What do we do, Soyuz? I don't see any other way forward.
While we're at it, we might as well check out the paintings on display, right?
I don't see why not. Let's look at them briefly.
(The furniture lining the hallway looks quite old. They must be antiques from the Imperiya era, before the Northern Parliament's inception.)
(I don't recognize these paintings, though. They seem valuable, but I have no idea when or where they're from.)
(Did the intruder put them here? Did they want to show us something?)
(Let's play along, I suppose. Perhaps these paintings will give us an insight into the intruder's character.)
Right. Follow me.
Soyuz's team walked down the corridor-turned-art gallery.
They looked at a wall with four paintings hanging on it, above which was a label with the words "Morning Sun" on it, suggesting a shared theme.
"Oil painting depicting all the factions signing a cease-fire agreement."
"The trains on which the agreement was signed were produced in a factory in St. Petersburg. They are seen as a symbol of victory."
A cease-fire agreement? When was this?
I think it's a painting from the Imperiya period.
Yet, I have no memory of an event like this ever happening.
"Oil painting depicting a high-speed train, the Star of the Imperiya, stopping at a station."
"Based on a well-known contemporary photograph. Looking at it brings one back to that radiant moment."
A well-known contemporary photograph?
Never seen anythin' similar to this. Ring any bells, Ognevoy?
No, none at all!
I've never heard anything about a train named "Star of the Imperiya" in service back home, either!
"Oil painting depicting the formation of a new worldwide alliance."
"There are dozens of flags fluttering in the painting, among which one with a golden two-headed eagle is placed in the most eye-catching location."
...I don't like it. Feels weird.
"Oil painting depicting the main hall of the Victory Commemoration Museum."
"The hall has many works on display, including Observer's rigging."
So that's her rigging... I've never seen the real thing before.
I'm guessing this museum was built to celebrate victory against the Sirens.
The girls continued down the ornate corridor.
They saw another four paintings, seemingly grouped together under a label that said, "Setting Sun."
"Oil painting depicting city residents going shopping on a holiday."
"There is a line in front of the grocery store. The people are waiting to buy vodka and brown bread."
This one at least feels a bit familiar.
Yeah. Many years ago, there used to be frequent shortages because our shipping lanes were closed. Things are much better now that they've reopened.
Sights like this are a thing of the past, really. We have enough food to go around now.
"Oil painting depicting a trial."
"The person on trial appears to be a naval officer who attempted to direct a warship to St. Petersburg."
(A warship to St. Petersburg? You don't need to be very bright to figure out how that trial must've gone.)
Never heard of anything like that. Another made-up story?
It's strange how it was a warship, not a shipgirl... I wonder why someone thought to paint this.
"Abstract painting redone in an evocative style."
"The giant is drawn with rough lines whilst the people are drawn with well-defined lines. The two create a striking contrast."
This one is very... abstract.
It stands out among the crowd, that's for sure.
"Sketch with simplistic linework depicting a flag on a building being lowered on Christmas Eve."
"This work was drawn by a child and as such the details are hazy. However, one can still feel the heavy, solemn mood in this piece."
The hell? It's been neat oil paintings 'til now, and now there's a scribble on display?
I'm not sure why, but looking at it makes me feel uneasy...
Same. Rubs me the wrong way!
The group went even further down the art gallery.
Once again, there was a collection of four paintings sharing a theme, above which was a label that said, "Utopia."
"Long picture scroll depicting an Imperiya-period palace that stretches on forever."
"Among the crowds, you were able to find countless familiar faces."
"After many, many detours, you and your friends gathered here. It was as though nobody left in the first place."
"Long picture scroll depicting the moment a historic treaty was made."
"One can clearly make out each and every person who was present at the treaty's signing."
"Among many flags fluttering in the wind, you saw one with a golden two-headed eagle and a white bear."
"The words, 'From now on, we may enjoy everlasting peace' are written near the top of the scroll."
"Long picture scroll depicting a massive banquet that will last into eternity."
"The saying, 'All good things must come to an end' has become antiquated."
"You made the world yours. Making a banquet last forever is child's play to you."
"A doodle depicting a class in session at a school."
"You noticed that the teacher is a Siren."
"This is, of course, perfectly normal. We have made peace with them, so why shouldn't we let them teach our children?"
The label is on the money. This sure looks like a utopia.
A utopia is our goal, too! It's what we're all working toward!
Well... Yeah, but doin' that and makin' peace with the Sirens are kinda mutually exclusive.
Soyuz, what do you think about all these paintings?
I can appreciate the author's ideas. It's not strange why someone would want to preserve these works of art.
As for the subjects themselves, some of them warrant caution whilst some others are plainly absurd.
Art is art, so I don't think my personal opinion of them matters much.
Uh-huh. And beyond that?
Beyond that?
If you're asking me about the author, then frankly, I can't make any kind of guess as to who that might be.
More precisely, I don't have enough information to work with.
I think we've spent long enough here. Let's head to the data center.
Wait, Soyuz–
...Hmph. Fine! Let's go!

Beneath the Glaciers.say: The Ring of the Wise. It was a doomsday bunker, built under the Antarctic ice inside an underground sea.
The shipgirls looked out across the breathtaking landscape. Save for Soyuz, who was involved with the construction, amazement welled in all their hearts.
Water as far as the eye could see below, a sky of shimmering blue ice overhead, and rock formations more striking than they had ever imagined.
They were feeling ecstatic, finally free from the bunker's sterile interiors.
It's so cute... No, I mean, so beautiful!
Beautiful ain't it, either! WEIRD is what it is! Don't forget that this is underground!
Why the hell are there mountains and a sea down here? There's even a sky, a horizon, and some kinda mist... This is freaky no matter how you cut it.
"Underground" isn't quite right. Technically, we're under an ice sheet.
As for the "sea," it likely came about from ice melting inside this natural cave.
It's not wrong to say that this place is strange, though, because it really is.
What's over there, where the mist is all thick?
And what's under the surface? How deep's the water? Does anythin' live down there?
Are there any living fossils? You know, like coelacanths!
I don't know.
We never did investigate the surrounding area much since the bunker has lay unused for the most.
If by any chance the intruder scanned it, we can hope to learn more about this place.
...That is, assuming they didn't delete everything.
At any rate, the Ring's data center is in the building just ahead. Let's hurry.
Hahah! Feels good to finally sprint across the water again!
Time to "see" what this sea has got in store for us!

sequence: Ring of the Wise - Ring Area

SOS.say: Phew. Fortunately, I've managed to recover some of the data.
That's great news. So, what'd you find?
I learned a few things about the geography of the snowmelt area and the manufacturing of those crystals.
I can confirm now that the crystals were indeed produced inside HL-17-36 and transported to the surface.
As for the snowmelt area, the bunker's heat dissipation system was behind it after all. The heat was directed toward the area after a certain amount of crystals had been delivered to the surface.
Therefore, whoever produced these crystals also melted the ice around the dropoff point so they would inevitably be discovered.
So they went out of their way to make it look like an anomalous phenomenon.
But why? Why do any of this?
Most likely to spark a conflict between factions, using the crystals as bait. Or maybe they wanted to expose the bunker's existence to the world to block the Azur Lane from reforming.
As I was about to say... I also found info on some scan scans the intruder ran.
Apparently, before they even started producing the crystals, they released underwater exploration drones into this sea.
It stands to reason they were looking for something under the water. They left behind quite a lot of survey data.
The drones initially sent in regular monitoring data. Initially.
After some point, the feeds started going offline, and as time passed, eventually all connection with the drones was lost.
Currently, there are zero operational drones in the water.
That can't be a coincidence. I think something attacked them.
Maybe something that's been living in this place since it formed millions of years ago...
So we ARE dealin' with a million-year-old sea monster...
Roooar!
AAAAAHHHH!
What was that for?! You're so mean!
You two just looked sooo cute, eyes sparkling, and I couldn't help myself!
Don't call me "cute," asshat!
Ahahaha! Don't be so mad! Come here and let me apologize by way of a big hug.
Okay, I forgive you!
I don't want a damn hug!
Ahahahaha♪
The girls' silly antics helped to lighten the tense mood.
Meanwhile, the adults had a look around. Poltava found a tablet, which she gave to Soyuz.
It looks like it's been used. It was just lying there on the table.
Interesting... It might have been the intruder. Let me have a look.
Unsurprisingly, the intruder had put a lock on the tablet.
Then, Soyuz got an idea. She could use her administrator access.
She tried to lift the lock on the device, and then...
Again, her surroundings all changed in an instant.
She took it surprisingly well. After all, she had already experienced it once before when she touched the crystal.
Or maybe she had simply accustomed to it – these tremors in reality – that she had been getting ever since she left the research station.
Whichever it was, she wasn't too shocked... until she saw that tower.
Is that... a Siren's mainframe?!
Across the horizon, a black tower with a frightening red eye reached high into the heavens.
Light radiated from its crown, spreading out across the sky like great ripples in a lake.
Following Compiler's defeat, Soyuz studied the information that the Commander had shared with her.
She was certain of what she was looking at. This was the inside of a Mirror Sea where a mainframe was stored.
She was also certain of – and in shock from – the one right in front of her.
A rigging shaped like an octopus grabbed hold of Soyuz with its tentacles.
Its apparent master, a dainty little girl at its center, extended a hand toward her.
You're... Observer!
It felt like pressure was acting on her from every direction. Her body couldn't move as she wanted.
I can't move... My rigging... Why can't I move?
You... It's your jamming, isn't it?
Shit... The tentacles are crushing me... Can't breathe... I...
Just as she was on the brink of falling unconscious, Soyuz looked at Observer's face.
Hidden behind her many tentacles, she looked as though... she was in pain.
She's... hurting?
The target of her jamming was in fact not Soyuz – but rather the tablet.
"SOS"... She's asking... for help?
An inordinate amount of time passed. Then, the darkness gave way to light.
Soyuz sharpened her focus and saw Poltava, handing her the tablet.
The vision was just that – a vision. Her reality was untouched.
...I'm back again.
"Back"?
Back from where, hmm?
Nowhere. Forget I said it.
You okay, Soyuz? You dropped the tablet and looked like you saw a ghost. What happened?
I had another hallucination. Don't worry, I'll be fine. I just need to rest a bit...
After going through the tablet's contents, Soyuz sat down on a chair to compose her thoughts.
(Was that Observer's mainframe? Iron Blood has said the likelihood is high that her mainframe is somewhere in the Southern Ocean...)
(But that can't be... right? This is too good to be a coincidence. Bismarck, Friedrich...)
(So much of our intel is uncertain. Still, I need to start forming theories.)
(There are three potential suspects for who the intruder is. A Siren, a META, or whoever controlled that Arbiter during the near-superimposition event.)
(They all possess advanced technology. It's also not far-fetched to say they could breach the security here with ease... but my only lead is Observer.)
(The odds that she'd disclose her mainframe's whereabouts of her own volition are as good as zero. So if it's not her, it's someone who's trying to gather everyone here.)
(That's the most plausible theory I've got right now...)
(I don't know who, but SOMEONE has pinpointed the location of her mainframe.)
(This person must've used the bunker's drones to try to find a way inside the Mirror Sea that hosts the mainframe.)
(For one reason or another, they didn't succeed, so they're trying something new – assembling all the factions here.)
(They produced those crystals, brought them to the surface, and leaked intel to Naval HQ to mobilize the Eagle Union. Outside shipgirls would get involved and be used as scapegoats...)
(Ugh... No, this isn't enough. The theory has too many holes.)
(Maybe Observer brought those crystals into existence and is asking for us to rescue her.)
(Maybe someone is showing me these visions to guide our attention to Observer for some reason that would benefit them.)
(Maybe the Sirens are behind it all. They could be trying to turn us against each other to keep us away from Antarctica.)
(What if they're putting us in danger to lure out Comrade Commander...)
(Using the bunker as a means to guide us – to guide the first party to discover the truth...)
(Is that it? Could that be... why Pamiat' is here...)
(I'm not sure of anything anymore. Whatever's really going on here, we can't deal with this situation with this few people.)
(We've got to get out of here immediately and tell the world what we've seen!)
Poltava!
Yes? What is it? You look pale.
The snowmelt area, the crystals... I think this is all one big trap.
We need to return to the surface... No, we need to retreat from Antarctica entirely!

sequence: "Young chicken stuffed with truffles, lamb saddle with baby vegetables, Bohemian pheasant spit roast."
"Foie gras soufflé, strawberry timbale, Victoria punch."
"The wonderful banquet begins, and the guest of honor takes her seat."
"I hope that this reception may satisfy you."
"If it does, won't you please stay forever?"
Around the time of Soyuz's arrival in Antarctica

The Commander's Support.say: Good news, Commander! I just got word from the Tribunal that the Northern Parliament has reached their Antarctic research station!
But the bad news is, all connection has been lost with the Tribunal's on-site monitoring equipment...
What? So they have no way of tracking them?
Seems so. For some reason, all their equipment across Antarctica broke down at the same time.
Clemenceau said it was probably due to jamming. It can't be a coincidence that they ALL went down simultaneously.
Then it's back to the drawing board for me. I'll figure something out.
Got it. Good luck.
Fleets from all factions are already departing for Antarctica...
If something happens there and we have no way of knowing about it, it'll be a disaster. So, to make sure that doesn't happen...
Location Unknown
I close my eyes, and a field of stars spread out before me.
...So, this is the encrypted META channel.
She really has been keeping an eye on me this whole time through her "cognitive interference".
You've come, Commander. Have you finally decided to make your move?
My hand has been forced. I can't leave the situation in Antarctica to boil over.
Everything was going according to plan... but some bad news just came in.
All contact between Antarctica and the outside world has been cut off. Is that right?
Correct. Do you think this might be Observer's doing, given her proficiency in electronic interference?
Not quite. This isn't Observer's doing.
But, I still might be able to help you out. Give me a sec.
Appreciate it. But, if it isn't Observer behind this, then who?
You'll find out soon enough.
I've been hesitant to ask, but... Have you known who the mastermind is from the very beginning?
Naturally. I know who they are, what they've done, and what they're after.
But...
But you can't tell me yet, can you?
I'm sorry... This is something you must uncover yourself.
It's alright, I shouldn't even have brought it up. Some things are better left in the dark, after all.
There's something else I need to take care of too. Can you provide me with a line directly to Saratoga?
As long as we can link up with the Eagle Union fleet, we should be able to keep the situation stable even if enemies show up.
...Isn't there something else you want to ask for?
Remember, Saratoga took this step because she was sure it would help Lexington.
What do you mean?
Don't you want to save Lexington?
Yes. Of course I do.
But I can't ask for your help with that. It's something I, myself, have to do.
...Very well.
Initiating strategic interface. Monitoring target: Antarctica. Setting up command system. Connected.
It's time to wake up, Commander.
I feel bad about borrowing Helena META's powers to do this... but at least this should improve the situation.
Let's see if this command system works despite the massive distance between us.
Helena, Memphis, check something for me. Is the command system over the Antarctic region connected?
Can you tell where everyone is?
Yes, it's connected! I'm seeing the info!
Wow, the tactical map is updating in real time... There's even a log of recent movements from every faction's fleets.
Is all this intel pulled from the Tribunal's monitoring equipment? Sheesh, they patched up their network in no time.
This is amazing. You can command just as well from here as if you were on the scene!
(Psst. How did you get this? Did you... ask the other Helena for help?)
(Yeah. Just a little.)
Right, so, we should check where everyone is right now.
Of course. I'll relay the feed to your monitor.
The Northern Parliament has entered the snowmelt area. They've not moved from there for a while, so they must be conducting an investigation on-site.
The Eagle Union iiis... Oh, Saratoga's group is heading for the snowmelt area.
It's looking like everyone's going there in the end, albeit in different orders.
Looks like the Royal Navy is still out on the ocean. They'll probably reach land soon.
On the other hand, Iron Blood hasn't budged from its position. They're still just patrolling the outskirts of the Southern Ocean.
Also, they sent a message about "encountering an unidentified enemy" from their Antarctic base. Maybe that has something to do with it?
Unidentified enemy, eh... Can you confirm if Königsberg is alright?
Sadly not. Still can't connect to the facility.
Okay. Keep monitoring. We'll gather as much intel as we can before we make a move.

A Banquet.say: Phew... We made it back to the surface...
I'm surprised by how quick that was. The emergency exit took us straight here.
That's what emergency exists are for. Time is of the essence during emergencies.
We shoulda just taken this route in the first place.
We couldn't. You can only get the route open from the inside for obvious security reasons.
Whatever. So what do we do now? Head back to Tallinn?
Yes. Then we'll–
Agh!
(Here it comes again...)
Soyuz? What's wrong?
Nothing, I'm okay. Let's take it one thing at a time.
First, we should get back to the research station. After that–
–we'll contact the... others...
Ahahah. How do you like the chicken with truffles?
It's among my favorites, and I've had many of the Imperiya's finest banquet dishes.
Where... am I...?
Soyuz suddenly found herself sitting at the end of a long table lined for a banquet.
To her front, an assortment of various luxurious dishes. To her sides, palace ornamentation that shone in the candlelight.
(A banquet... in an Imperiya-era palace?)
(I shouldn't be here... I should be with them, evacuating immediately...)
(But... how? Where is the way out?)
(From the Ring of the Wise... take the submarine and go north...)
(Now I get it... The art gallery. It has been whispering to me...)
Hm? What's the matter? You don't like it?
Alright, then try this turtle soup. I'm sure it'll hit the spot and clear your mind!
I see... I'll help myself, then.
Thoughts rushed through Soyuz's head. She picked up a spoon, felt it in her hand, and looked at the soup.
She took a small spoonful and let it rest in her mouth.
(I can taste it.)
(This is no mere hallucination... Or perhaps, this space exists at a level far beyond the sensory realm to begin with...)
Heehee. You must like it, judging from the look on your face.
...I do. It tastes very good.
Pamiat' – if that's who you are – what is this place? Why is there a banquet?
In order – this is my palace, and why the heck not? You're my guest, and I'm being a good host.
Whaddya think? Pretty nice place, right?
I've... never been treated to something like this before.
Right? And there's so much more to do here than just enjoying the endless banquet!
You can walk among flowers, hunt on the ice fields, and play on the beach, just to name a few.
Don't you want to stay here?
The thoughts in Soyuz's head were starting to feel clearer and clearer.
Delusions crept up on her. That this was reality. That the snowscape she was just in was the real hallucination.
I could... see myself staying here...
But... what about the others?
I can bring them here if you want me to. Then you can all enjoy the eternal Paradise together.
Eternal... Paradise...
Paradise. Heaven. Elysium. The word was like a calming whisper from Mother Nature.
Another world superimposed itself upon Soyuz's vision.
A world where everyone danced hand-in-hand. A world where flowers bloomed and vodka flowed freely.
A world that will drown me if I let my guard down...
Which is why... I need to snap out of it.
Snap out of what?
This. A world this perfect doesn't exist – not yet.
This place is pure escapism. Its only purpose is to make people give up and stop dreaming of better things.
Those who buy into it have no commitment to fight for humanity, and they certainly are no comrades of mine.
Pamiat's world seemed to tremble. A crack spread across her utopia.
But isn't this exactly the kind of world you're fighting for?
Who cares if it's real or not! It doesn't matter if it's escapism!
You can save the people you care about, Soyuz! Take them away from the fighting and the suffering and let them live in happiness forever!
Who do you take me for? Someone who'd give up on her ideals and her duty to wallow in a fantasy?
No! It's not giving up on anything! It's just taking a shortcut to your destination!
Don't you see it?! Your dream, your duty, is to create a world like this!
All I see is a person who doesn't understand anything.
No, I guess I don't! Because YOU don't make any sense to me!
I once knew people who talked big. About dreams, ideals, and missions. They all sacrificed themselves.
And you know what they got in return? NOTHING!
Even if self-sacrifice for your ideals doesn't result in immediate change, it will invariably lead to a brighter future.
You will never reach that future if nobody makes difficult choices.
But who says you and your comrades have to be the sacrifice?
...I don't understand you. You're always the same, no matter the era.
There's nothing wrong in living for your own sake, in wanting a future where you and only those you care about survive...
Isn't it time you showed who you really are, "Pamiat' Merkuria"?
Aww, you still saw through me so easily.
Show me. What is your true identity?
Ahahaha! Come on. You already know...
...Who's under the mask.

A Touch of Snow.say: You're... Pamiat' Merkuria META.
I told you you already knew.
The girl on the opposite side of the golden dining room spoke loudly and clearly.
I am Pamiat'. A Pamiat' from a story in the distant past.
What did you do to Pamiat' from my branch?
Hmm. Looks like the cognitive influence hasn't completely worn off yet.
Welp, seeing as it's not gonna work, might as well ditch the disguise altogether.
The air gets all stuffy in this palace. Why don't we pop outside?
In an instant, the golden palace vanished, being replaced by a plain of silvery snow stretching as far as the eye could see.
Soyuz's brain fog was blown away by a freezing breeze. Her mind cleared, she could now get a good look at the girl in front of her.
At the girl who suddenly appeared when they arrived in Antarctica. The girl who had no reason to be here.
Finally, she realized the source of the unrest brewing inside her–
Pamiat' is in Europa right now. Right by Comrade Commander's side.
And you, you must've entered the submarine as soon as it crossed into Antarctica.
Heehee. That's right, you've been talking to me this whole time.
It was quite fun, you know~ Pretending we were all friends and stuff.
...I didn't find it fun in the slightest.
What is your goal? What are you going to do to my friends?
What am I going to "do" to them? Why would I want that?
Is this the difference between how you treat your friends, and how you treat everyone else? What a terribly cold woman you are.
You're not "everyone else." You're my enemy.
I'm not in the mood for small-talk. Answer my question.
Ehhh? Enemy?
Just because I'm not YOUR Pamiat' doesn't make me your enemy, right?
C'mon, you're being way too paranoid. I haven't laid a finger on your friends.
Look – you're the only person who I've invited to my castle.
As for what my goal is... Actually, there are three things.
Only one main objective though. The other two are more like side quests.
But, spilling the beans outright would be terribly dull, so I'll have you guess instead.
I see. Then I've wasted enough time on you and I'm leaving this–
Wait!
Remember the art gallery? Did it seriously not make you feel ANYTHING?
Oh. So it was you who put the paintings there.
And it was you who set off these events – because you're the one who's after Observer's mainframe.
Ding, ding, ding!
Boy, using her as bait sure yielded a huge catch! Not that it matters. You're the only one I invited.
Why only me?
Because I'm interested in you.
I've heard how you preach about your motherland's ideals and beliefs, and I wondered if they'd change or not when you were exposed to a wider world.
The things in those paintings were all real. I saw and heard them with my own eyes and ears.
How does that make you feel, Soyuz?
I suppose you've lived a life full of high highs and low lows.
I wish I could see what your home world was like...
But more than anything else, your malicious intentions offend me.
Uh-huh. So, what's your answer to my invitation?
Do I even need to answer?
The wheels of time are always moving forward. New worlds are born, and old ones die. It's inevitable, unavoidable.
Your desire to linger in the past, to never move on and accept change, sounds insufferable.
"Always moving forward"... Sure, but nobody's at the wheel. Nobody gets to choose their own future.
What if we COULD choose, though?
So we could go back in time and go through the same mistakes?
Listen. The past is known. Just as there are mistakes, there is glory before the downfall.
There is no hope in stubbornly clinging on to the past.
It is our duty to face the future and march onward, not retreat inward.
"Face the future," "march forward," blah blah blah.
You sound exactly like my mortal enemy.
Tell me, what's wrong with a known past?
Abandoning the future is just abandoning uncertainty. The future doesn't always have hope in store, you know.
I've been to many places, and I've learned a lot on the way.
The Imperiya, the Northern Parliament, the Revolutionary Front... They all thrived, and then they all crashed and burned.
No matter who you are, no matter how hard you try, no dynasty will last forever as long as the future exists. You know it to be true.
No matter. My pledge is to the Northern Parliament, and I will keep fighting for its future.
Look, I get it. I used to be just like you.
But I know something. There is nothing in that future you're fighting so hard for.
It is empty... just like this blank snowscape.
So what's wrong with choosing the past if the future has nothing for you?
Why shouldn't I hold on to past glories, to when things were good?
What reason is there not to relive those happy moments with your friends in Paradise?
You call that "Paradise"?
Dropped into that single moment in time stretched to infinity, like a lab rat in a cage to be experimented on?
I refuse, "Pamiat'". We've already had to endure a life like that for far too long.
We were bound by the Reenactments, forced to go down a path that was not our own. Now that we forge our own future, we will never go back.
Even should this path lead to destruction, it is something that we created – and fought for. Our destiny is our own, and nothing can take that from us.
It is not something that can be so easily granted by others.
I believe your world was also treated as a test site by the Sirens, just as they see ours?
You can be the happiest little lab rat in the world, but in the end, you still exist only to be used. Surely, you must understand this.
The only way out is to break free from the rules thrust upon you. Seize your own destiny––
Stop talking as if you've got everything figured out!
You think we didn't fight hard enough, is that it?!
We fought, and fought, and what did it all amount to? Everyone but me is DEAD!
Look at me! There's nobody else left!
They protected me, and now I carry all their unfulfilled wishes with me!
What gives you the right to lecture me when you haven't seen a fraction of what I've had to endure?
Tell me, no matter how hard we fight, how are ordinary people supposed to overturn fate itself?
...I see. So this is the past that you were forced to endure, Pamiat'.
It is. And I was given the chance to choose one more time...
So, I chose to bring back the glory of the past – my home, and my friends.
In a Paradise that I rule over, where we can be together for all of eternity––
A Paradise that you rule over... The glory of the past...
You know, I heard someone say something very similar not that long ago.
Oh yeah? Then you've probably figured out what my first goal is.

Advent of Paradise.say: Oh yeah? Then you've probably figured out what my first goal is.
The confrontation in the world of snow continued on.
Realizing that Soyuz had figured out her goal, the so-called Pamiat' graciously revealed her true aims.
In that case...
In an instant, the ice cracked, and an enormous machine rose up.
A Gazer... The false gods...
You're trying to superimpose... the Divine Vestige onto our world?
That's not what it's called, silly.
Paradise, Elysium... Utopia. I've tried coming up with a lot of names, but none of them hit right.
What kind of name do you prefer?
Sometimes wrathful, other times playful, Pamiat' was as mercurial as her name suggested.
But at the same time, Soyuz knew that neither her words nor demeanor were genuine.
More than that, she knew that she couldn't keep letting Pamiat' string her along.
...There's no need for that, "Pamiat'".
You can change its name, but you cannot change its nature.
Booooring. I suppose you're tired of chatting with me, huh?
That's too bad. If words won't work, then I guess I'll have to default to force.
With those words, their conversation drew to a close.
Now then, I'll need a moment to get ready!
In the meantime... Feel free to play with my little friends~
Good luck~ and goodbye!
The table, Pamiat' Merkuria, and the previously encroaching Gazer all dispersed at once.
Soyuz was back in the mountains of Antarctica with her comrades.
It seemed that the argument had all happened in the space of an instant.
Pamiat's gone. I must assume she's finished chatting.
So, you were saying? We should get back to the research station and contact the others?
...Right.
Inform Iron Blood, the Eagle Union, and all other forces near Antarctica and surrounding waters that enemy forces have infiltrated and to retreat at once.
Understood. Should we... elaborate on who these enemy forces are?
Poltava and the others' slight confusion reminded Soyuz of what Pamiat' had said – that she'd only invited her. She breathed a sigh of relief.
...Tell them that an army of Gazers is on its way to attack Antarctica, please.
Gazers?! You mean the ones that attacked the World Expo–
KABOOOM!
With an all-encompassing boom, Gazers erupted from the ice, sending veritable hail flying forth.
Gazers really did come!
And a hell of a lot of 'em... Those things were hiding under the ice?!
Not necessarily, knowing the possibility of a superimposition... But this is surely too soon.
Poltava, send an abbreviated message: everyone, and I mean everyone, prepare to retreat.
Yes, ma'am!

United Front.say: The Antarctic tactical map filled up with glowing red dots, indicating the presence of hostiles.
In the blink of an eye, the Antarctic situation went from peaceful to bordering on crisis.
Commander! Soyuz just sent an unencrypted message! "Gazers are invading Antarctica!" I've verified the ID!
Gazers? Those giant weapons the Hierophant unleashed at the World Expo?
So they're back. This is really bad news.
Wait, didn't the Gazers emerge from the Divine Vestige last time? Marco Polo was manipulated into caking Europa with it.
If they're invading using the same method, that means someone else has spread out the Divine Vestige.
But who could it be?
Unless... It was Soyuz and her team...
No, it can't be them.
Marco Polo needed a long time to prepare everything. Soyuz's fleet didn't have that time – they arrived in Antarctica just a while ago.
Some other, unknown party did this.
Saratoga and the Operation Frozen Angel fleet are engaging the Gazers now.
Looks like they're still heading for the snowmelt area, albeit at a slower speed.
The Northern Parliament, on the other hand... they're LEAVING the snowmelt area, heading for the coast.
Ugh! What is Saratoga thinking?!
Commander, we can't wait any longer! Take control of the battlefield!
I'm going to. Set up a chain of command and establish a real-time communication line for me.
I'll contact Soyuz first to find out what's happening on the front!
Antarctica - Snowmelt Area
KABOOOM!
I've created an opening! Comrades, follow me!
Maintain speed even if you're injured! Head straight for the submarine's rendezvous point!
Yes, ma'am!
BEEP BEEP!
A call?
And it's from... Comrade Commander?!
The Commander's calling?! Why now, of all times?
Didn't Königsberg say everything's being jammed, and no communications can go in or out from Antarctica?
I suppose Comrade Commander found another way. This timing is too perfect, though...
Comrade Commander must have been monitoring our situation to be ready to take command at a moment's notice.
The reason we're the first to be contacted is probably because we possess intel from the frontlines.
Heehee... Very well. Let us fight together, Comrade Commander.
(The crystals being manufactured, Observer sending an SOS...)
(There being a secret bunker there... and another Pamiat' Merkuria.)
(That's it. I've pieced it all together now.)
(First, we know now that Observer's mainframe is somewhere under the Antarctic ice.)
(And, by some stroke of luck, the Ring of the Wise's substructure is physically connected to it.)
(At the same time, the Hierophant wanted to invade our branch, so she used the other Pamiat' to infiltrate the bunker and scan for Observer's mainframe.)
(Observer fought back, but it was no use, and she's now in mortal danger.)

(Left with no choice, she produced those crystals and left them somewhere we'd discover them. This would get Naval HQ's attention, bringing all the shipgirls in one place so they could resolve the crisis.)
(Saratoga sensed Naval HQ's movement and pursued the crystals, pinning her hopes on its rigging-enhancing properties...)
(Many in the Eagle Union, her included, saw the seriousness of the situation. This eventually resulted in a huge fleet mobilizing.)
(That's when I intervened, using the info Helena META gave me to stop this plot by sending intel about the crystal to Iron Blood and the Northern Parliament.)
(Iron Blood, knowing how Naval HQ would respond, stuck to watching diligently from the sidelines.)
(The Northern Parliament went in directly, however. Soyuz, who's suffering from an innate hull defect, organized a fleet and headed to Antarctica.)
(Then, Pamiat' Merkuria META, who'd been in Antarctica for a long time, made contact with Soyuz out of personal curiosity.)
(She accompanied them into the bunker, where she tried to persuade – or rather, cognitively influence – Soyuz, but she failed.)
(Once the jig was up, she activated the Divine Vestige she'd put down beforehand and summoned the Gazers...)
(Bravo, Soyuz. My hat is off to you for gathering this much intel in so little time.)
(Helena META deserves thanks, too. For helping me like she said she would.)
(If she really predicted everything I would do, then I'm officially scared of how well she knows me... Though, it's a blessing, too.)
Thanks, Helena. For having my back.
Now, then...
BEEP BEEP!
Commander, what's our next move? I'm guessing you're done talking to Soyuz.
Yeah. She gave me a rundown of the situation.
First of all, tell Clemenceau that we'll be using the Tribunal's base as a temporary command center.
Then contact Bismarck and tell her I have something urgent to talk to her about.
Finally, remember that we have Naval HQ's eyes on us. Take all the other staff to the Tribunal's base. I'll catch up with you there.
What? The Tribunal's base? You mean...?
You heard me. Also, set things up for confidential communication. I'll explain everything later.
Got it! I'll see what I can do!
Hanging up now. Good luck, Commander!
Alright...
Now to get in touch with Saratoga.

Frozen Angel.say: After organizing my thoughts for a bit, I sent Saratoga an encrypted call.
BEEP BEEP!
...Yes? Saratoga speaking!
Hey. Are you okay?
Huh?! W-wait, Commander?! How'd you...
Um, I'm actually kind of busy right now, so...
We need to talk about Frozen Angel.
Oh...
I had a feeling you'd find out sooner or later. Sorry for keeping you in the dark...
I should've known the truth would get out after such a large fleet was mobilized.
How did you get through the jamming all across the Southern Ocean?
Actually, no, never mind.
You have your ways. You probably already know what's going on here...
I also know your fleet is fighting the Gazers.
You've got your hands full, I know, but this is important.
You must know by now that the crystals are part of a Siren plot.
Y-yeah...
Then why are you still doing this?
If you KNOW that the crystals are bait, why are you still walking into their trap?
Then there's how you handled Constellation's experiment in the Sea of Stars. It's like you haven't been yourself for a while now.
You never meant to put anyone in danger, I know that, but why are you trying to bear responsibility for everything?
Something has clearly happened. I want you to tell me.
Commander, you're still in Europa, aren't you?
That's good... That means you're in a safe place.
Listen... I know there's a chance that Frozen Angel is an empty hope. I know that, of course.
I knew all along that Wisdom Cubes and those crystals were completely different.
But, even if they don't work like Cubes do, maybe – just maybe – they have the power to fix her hull.
Lex has been debilitated ever since that battle, and we've still not found any way to cure her.
And while we don't know any cure, maybe the Sirens or whoever controls them does.
You have to understand... I'm desperate, Commander. I need something that can save her.
So desperate I was willing to walk into a trap all on my own... But people came with me, and I couldn't just tell them it was a trap...
And now they're all here, and it's my fault... Because I didn't have the courage to tell them...
Listen to me. You're not the only one to whom Lex is important.
She taught me so much when I was studying at the academy, and she was always there during my stint in the Eagle Union.
She isn't just a shipgirl under my command. She's a friend and a person close to my heart.
We made a promise, you and I. To save Lexington, whatever it took. I would never go back on that promise.
So trust me when I say this – you can't carry this burden all by yourself.
But... It's too late now...
Lexington's condition is stable. We WILL find a way to cure her.
But the sun, it's... N-never mind.
Ahem! Commander, I really, reeeally need your help!
It'd be one thing if we were fighting mass-produced Sirens, but a swarm of Gazers this big is too much for our fleet to deal with!
Remember your lesson on the Antarctic Treaty? It lets you command not just us shipgirls...
But also all the defensive weaponry that exists around Antarctica!
...Thanks for trusting me, Sara.
Of course. I've always trusted you!
Okay, I'm handing control of our fleet over to you now!
Do your thing and lead us to victory!

An Exchange.say: As I expected, Clemenceau immediately approved my request to borrow the Tribunal's base.
I finished my call with Saratoga and went to the command center. There, I saw that the Eagle Union and Northern Parliament were already done setting up a chain of command.
Commander, I set up a new rendezvous route for Saratoga's and Soyuz's fleets, like you asked.
Also, the research station is orchestrating an evacuation as we speak. It's going smoothly so far.
Good. Then I can finally breathe for a bit.
*clap clap clap clap*
Winning a battle thousands of kilometers away. Imagine that.
This has been a magnificent display of your legendary leadership skills, Commander. If you ever retire and become a politician, you'll be the face of your party.
You're exaggerating. I've not "won the battle" yet, I've only just begun repositioning fleets.
I'm not proud about the distance thing, either. If I could, I'd teleport to Antarctica right now.
But enough about me. Have you done the Bismarck thing?
Yes. Just as you asked, I've prepared a closed comms channel to her. That's what I came here to tell you.
It's over in our secret room. Nobody else will be able to hear what you say.
Nobody else besides you, I'm guessing.
Heehee. I don't know what you mean♪
She's on the line, but I can reschedule the call if now is a bad time for you.
Now is as good a time as any. Lead the way.
Of course. Right this way.
Tribunal Headquarters - Secret Room
This is not how I was hoping we'd meet again, but things are what they are. Greetings.
While we have publically spoken face-to-face before, well, as you may know...
Showing my face in the capacity of a leader in a private conversation is... quite different.
I will say, I'm surprised you'd go out of your way to contact me like this.
If I'm to guess, this is about transferring command down in Antarctica, right?
What? Did Clemenceau not tell you anything?
All she told me was that this was about sharing intel and cooperating in Antarctica. Her tone was quite "figure out the rest yourself."
It sounds like she withheld what she knew from you as well.
That sounds like Clemenceau alright.
Long story short, here's what we're dealing with...
It was YOU who relayed the discovery of that Antarctic crystal to me and Soyuz?
So you've converted Clemenceau of all people to your side?
You never cease to amaze, Commander.
It's no wonder why Friedrich would sing your praises on a daily basis. Hahahah.
Unfortunately, I couldn't predict that we'd run into Gazers.
If they're not dealt with, we'll have a disaster on our hands on par with the World Expo... No, maybe even bigger.
On the bright side, we've fought them once before, and the superimposition hasn't begun yet.
Manifesting a concept like Marco Polo did then will be much harder on Antarctica. Even if it does happen, the concept's foothold should be weak.
If all factions engage it as one, we have a fighting chance.
That, I agree with.
Obviously, opinions differ from one faction to the next. But we need to set those aside and coordinate our efforts.
For that reason, I would like to take command of your Southern Ocean fleet.
Of course, I could also use any other forces you have in reserve. Knowing you, I'm certain you have some at the ready.
I do. I moved some people around as soon as I received word about the jamming.
Here's what's going to happen.
I will give you immediate control over our forces in not just this battle, but all future battles as well.
This, I hope, will help you forge the future that you dream of.
All future battles? You're allowing me to control whenever I see fit?
Thank you so much, Bismarck. This will help greatly.
You're welcome. As long as you're up for the task, you can give orders to our shipgirls on the front whenever you want.
Use their strength as though it were your own.
Right. I will not abuse this power.
Thank you. Until next time.
Tribunal Headquarters - Corridor
Oh, you got done sooner than I expected.
Seems like it went exceedingly well. Color me impressed.
Who could have predicted she'd give you THAT much leeway, hmm?
Was I, really? You can never be sure.
Heehee. I'll take that as a compliment♪
But enough joking around. I got in touch with the Royal Navy's fleet.
It seems they anticipated the Gazers' arrival, though they're still scrambling to figure out what to do.
PTSD from the events of the World Expo, perhaps.
Won't you be a dear and get them to calm down?
Remember, you can always pull the Antarctic Treaty on them.
Say something like – "As the Commander of the Azur Lane, I have unrivaled authority over Antarctica and its ocean."
"I request that you transfer control of your fleet to me immediately and that you cooperate with other fleets under my command until the battle is over."
Not to be pushy or anything, but I could send that message on your behalf.
No thanks. I'll speak to them myself.
Aww. That's too bad♪

Independent Variables.say: Thanks to the hard work of Helena META as well as the shipgirls on-site, the jamming in Antarctica had been reduced to all but a non-issue.
The command system was now getting everyone's positions, compositions, and statuses. Stable strategic communication had become possible.
All faction fleets reorganized, creating a single, unified fleet under my command – the Azur Lane fleet.
Reinforcements came in, too. Not just the ones Bismarck had prepared, but backup from the Eagle Union as well.
The excuse given for the latter's presence was that, "They just so happened to be in the Southern Ocean." Yeah, right...
Sara – or should I say, Miss Saratoga – really brought her entire fan club.
She even got Enterprise's friends to come all the way out here...
Ahem. Anyway...
Let's start with reviewing the evac from Antarctica.
The Eagle Union's and Royal Navy's bases are far from the action and their staff have already withdrawn.
Königsberg is headed for the NP's research station right now. She'll evacuate after linking up with Soyuz's group.
The fleets attached to each station will escort their members to safety. They won't partake in the operation until the evacuation is complete.
On the Divine Vestige front, there have been no sightings of the cocoon that brought forth the Arbiter last time.
The Gazers have been spreading the Divine Vestige, but only at a glacial pace compared to the time of the World Expo.
So far, my theory has been correct.
Pamiat' Merkuria META didn't plan ahead like Marco Polo did. She's apparently just been winging it.
She is the key to all this. If we can neutralize her, it should end the crisis right there and then.
As long as we stop the superimposition, we can take our time neutralizing the Gazers and drones.
Luckily for us, it seems Soyuz has a big fan in Pamiat'. She's been pursuing them this whole time.
All while ignoring how I've been rapidly redeploying our forces.
All we need to do is lure her into an ambush and we can wipe out her and her Gazer army in one fell swoop.
Soyuz's team is on the case, moving from the snowmelt area and toward the ambush point.
With the Union's air support on our side, everything should go according to plan.
Hell, the trap should be even more effective if we can push Pamiat's buttons and get her to run into our ambush completely blind.
According to Soyuz, the girl is pretty emotionally unstable. It shouldn't take much to agitate her.
Alright. Forces lying in ambush... Iron Blood has Brünhilde, Hindenburg, and Felix Schultz, to name a few.
On the Union side, Saratoga's fleet is also en route to the ambush site.
They've got battleships Washington, North Carolina, and the Colorado sisters. For carriers, they're bringing Saratoga herself of course, Ranger, and Independence.
Add to that a few dozen cruisers and destroyers.
Over in the Southern Ocean, there's the Royal Navy's Southern Ocean fleet led by Implacable, as well as an Iron Blood support fleet with Prinz Heinrich among the ranks.
Finally, there's the Union backup fleet that "just happened to be there"...
All in all, we have a really good shot at victory.
Antarctica - Ice Fields
Soyuz, do you read me? What's your status?
Damages are within estimates. We will reach the destination momentarily.
The air support is almost there! Just hang in a little longer!
I appreciate the support, it's just... aircraft will only deal quite limited damage to the Gazers.
Who's saying I've only brought aircraft?
I've got a whole arsenal of stuff ready! You'll see later!
Soyuz, our fleet is in position. All there is to do now is wait for the target to come.
And the reinforcements are coming in at full speed!
C'mon, Eisen! Faster, boy!
...I'm Bunker Hill. Eagle Union Southern Ocean fleet. En route at max speed to objective.
Same with the Royal Navy's Southern Ocean fleet. We'll arrive in three hours. It's an honour to be fighting alongside you.
This is Ingraham. We're done evacuating all the Union and Royal Navy forces. The escort fleets will now join you in battle.
NP's and IB's evacs aren't finished yet. I'm working on it as fast as I can.
Thank you all for the status reports.
We'll continue to our destination and report back when we've arrived.
Sheesh. The Commander is leading everyone flawlessly, despite being across the world.
Yes. And under circumstances not even I could have predicted...
But... that's what makes Comrade Commander so reliable, don't you think?
I shall be the bait for Pamiat'. Everyone else, please follow the orders you've been given.

Glimmer in the Snowscape.say: Nighttime fell upon Antarctica, and the showdown was drawing near.
An aurora shone overhead whilst the grand chase finally came to a close.
Well, well. I was wondering why you stopped fleeing. Of course you had an ambush prepared.
Eisen, that's the bad guy! Let's get her!
Get a whiff of that one. Smells like danger.
And what are we, harmless little kittens? We're just as dangerous.
Heard you've been starting shit here in Antarctica. Get ready to pay the price!
The Knights of the Sea are done letting you run rampant!
It's not too late to stop. Surrender peacefully and we might just let you off with a stern beating.
Wooow. Those are big words for a bunch of mice drawn to the big cheese.
Observer couldn't have predicted this would happen. Not in a million years.
I mean, of course she couldn't. Being disconnected from your mainframe does that to you.
...She has lost connection to her mainframe?
What, you didn't know?
C'mon. Put the pieces together.
Do you think Observer would've just let Compiler get terminated if she was operating at normal capacity?
She cut herself off from her OWN mainframe. Can you believe that?
Something in your branch has made her go cuckoo, methinks.
She hasn't connected to her mainframe for a very long time now. Why? I don't know.
But at any rate, she can't get updates, can't access the Natural Calculation System, and can't use the resources of Laplace's Demon.
The only things she can use are her mainframe's nodes and her vessels.
Like, at this point, why would she even bother? She can't do jack!
It's no wonder, then, that she hasn't shown herself for so long.
Has she really become so weak she can't even deal with a threat right next to her mainframe?
That's right.
The funniest thing of all is that she thought she could hide it from me!
You can't hide a weakness as glaring as that! Hahahaha!
I would like it if the enemy of my enemy were my friend... but you don't seem keen on an alliance.
You clearly don't like Observer either, though. Why don't you take it out on her and we can all part on peaceful terms?
No can do. In case you forgot, I've got things to do here.
(Right, her three goals... Laying the Divine Vestige was only one of them.)
We have stopped an invasion such as this once. We will do it again.
Whatever your plans are, they will not come to fruition. This I can promise you.
Really, huh? Let's put your promise to the test. I mean, I've already come this far!
If you wanna stop me then you'd better give me your best shot! Ahahaha!

Unto Dawn.say: KABOOOM!
With a loud explosion, another Gazer toppled over.
Thirty minutes had elapsed since the battle began. Already, the shipgirls had gained an overwhelming advantage.
This Pamiat' is just a weak imitation!
She doesn't stand a chance against our firepower!
That's 'cuz we've got, like, a million people whalin' on her! It helps that we've got the Commander and ME, of course!
But isn't she a little TOO weak? You'd think the head honcho would put up more of a fight!
Compared to that World Expo hag on her flyin' throne, yeah, I guess she's kind of a pushover.
I think the Tribunal deployed a superweapon to deal with her in that case.
In this case, though... it almost doesn't feel right with how easily she's going down...
(It IS odd. Beyond using Gazers as meat shields, she is making no attempt to avoid incoming fire.)
(Rationally speaking, she's only digging her own grave... Yet, I remember one of Kronshtadt's reports described a fighting style just like this.)
(One "Bon Homme Richard" similarly wasn't afraid of damage, and used it to erode the minds of her attackers...)
(No... Could she be doing the same?)
Pamiat', why are you fighting so hard for your obsession with the past?
I can understand looking back on the past and basking in nostalgia. What I cannot understand is trying to replace the future with the past.
You've suffered a lot, fought hellish battles, but at the very least, you survived.
As a survivor, you should bear the hopes of your fallen comrades. You should live on, and fight for the good of all of us.
Look what you're doing now. Do you think they'd be proud of you?
You are trampling on not just everything they fought for, but everything YOU fought for as well.
Hahahaha! "Obsession"? Do I look like someone driven by an obsession?
No! I'm doing this because it's what my fallen comrades would have wanted!
Haha... Ahahaha...
AHAHAHAHAHAHAH!
Pamiat's terrifying laughter rang through everyone's communicators. It sounded wrong, like someone was laughing THROUGH her.
Oh, gosh! Do you hear that?! It's like her laughter's coming from inside my head!
Is it just me, or is it gettin' cold as hell all of a sudden?
Condensation is forming on my chains... Soyuz, I think she's healing herself!
Not on my watch, she isn't!
KABOOOM!
Soyuz fired a volley at the girl possessed by some evil spirit.
The shells flew through the air, but stopped short of their target, plunging into the sea like rocks.
A pulse of sheer cold radiated outward from Pamiat', freezing everything in its path at an unbelievable rate.
The sea froze faster than the shipgirls could react. One by one, they were frozen in place.
...So this was your plan all along.
You knowingly walked into our trap and allowed us to attack you.
Ahaha... A small sacrifice to make, to be able to finally come into contact with the font of essence.
By plucking the fruit from the prime branch, I have at last found... the true past!
Look at this – the Revolutionary Front! All I ever knew about it was what little that one person told me.
And now... it's like I was actually there myself.
Ahahaha... I see it in the fragments... Very, very interesting.
Wouldn't you know it – your Ring of Whatever isn't just a doomsday bunker.
It's also where you store that device based on data you recovered from the RF.
If it wasn't for that, you wouldn't have built a bunker all the way out here. Isn't that right, Soyuz?
Ahahaha! Oh, what a laugh!
And you called ME driven by obsession! You should have a good look in the mirror!
Project Dawn is the biggest obsession of them all!
You think it's your ray of hope? You couldn't be more wrong. It's an express ticket to your downfall.
Just being close to it brings misfortune.
Do you know what happened to the people involved in this project? I can tell you.
Don't answer. I'll tell you either way.
